evidence.text: benefits, pay and stocks. bonus and unlimited pto. Take all your time Good pay, everything else sucks
Really great benefits, they do wellbeing days off every 1-2 months, HSA contributions, good health insurance, a nice gym, and the cafe food has some good stuff. Great benefits
Great co-workers Company shut down week of July 4th Company shut down between Christmas and New Years Overworked
Love some of my co-workers. Benefits and time off generous. Amazing benefits, downsides of a panicked corporaton

Benefits are incredible - unlimited PTO, one week off in July and December, good selection of medical and dental, great parental leave. After 5 years you can take a month long sabbatical. Fantastic benefits
Benefits and leave policies. Work life balance Excellent benefits and good work life balance
Great culture and people. Maternity leave canât be beat. Amazing work life balance

Good pay Great benifits Good work life balance Good work life balance

evidence.role: Former employee - Senior Partner Program Manager
evidence.text: Great salary and benefits package Limited Advancement for Experienced (Older)/Minorities/Women at Adobe
Great company culture, excellent benefits, strong brand reputation, innovative products, and talented colleagues Great Company, But Limited Career Growth

â¢ Adobe remains a solid company overall, with strong and competent leadershipâShantanu and the C-suite executives are respected. â¢ Human creativity still matters, giving Adobe relevance despite rising competition. â¢ Management generally has deep institutional knowledge. Adobeâs Still Solid, but Internal Culture and Competition Are Taking a Toll

-Supportive management within the Marketo business -Strong sense of community and camaraderie -Lots of growth opportunities, education-wise -Certifications galore to earn while working -Autonomy on project work and driving initiatives that you're passionate about -Communicative teams that work well together cross-functionally -Really great culture all-around Mixed Feelings- acquisition impact (Marketo)

Working at Adobe as a People Analyst was an enriching experience that allowed me to delve deep into the heart of the company's culture. From day one, I was immersed in a dynamic environment where collaboration and innovation were at the forefront. As a People Analyst, I had the privilege of not only analyzing data but also deciphering the intricate patterns of human behavior within the organization. The culture at A...

Great benefits and pay is competitive (maybe slightly on the lower side compared to FAANG). Most employees and managers are good, kind people. Culture is very friendly, positive, nice. Very supportive of people with families or anyone needing special accommodations. Bureaucratic but great benefits

Adobe is all about the individual employee! You'll find amazing teams who are all eager and willing to help each other! We work together to continue to drive Adobe as the global leading creative software company! Adobe is an AMAZING company to work for

evidence.role: Current employee - Principal Product Marketing Manager
evidence.text: Great collaborative culture and benefits. Supportive management that helps navigate career. Great work environment!
Benefits & unlimited PTO (depends on manager) Highly toxic and optics driven environment
Good job and pay for entry-level but promo path is super hard. Stable job but hard to grow
Good Benefits, Amazing Team, Hybrid work Stable Job Great Company
800 lb gorilla in digital marketing Good place to be if you can tolerate the politics
Great wlb, non-toxic work culture (depends on team tho), fun work if you want to Great WLB and fun to work - if you like

The hybrid work policy is a bit fuzzy right now, it seems like something leadership is scrutinizing but individual orgs are pushing their own philosophies. *most* offices are a bore to attend, so that makes for difficult motivation to visit regularly. Exceptions are San Jose and San Francisco. Not a single bad thing to say

There are several male senior leaders who have been brought in who hold a very misogynistic approach to working with female leaders. Certain types of toxic behaviors - a Senior Leader who will only work with a male team member and dismisses working with any female member is not addressed. It is rewarded and this misogynistic and inequitable behavior creates a lot of opportunities for many male members of the team an...

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Software Engineer L4
evidence.text: No stocks, salary growth ~2-4% annually No growth

evidence.role: Current employee - Sr Product Marketing Manager
evidence.text: DMe has a horrible reputation. Doesn't allow for growth for Women or POC. No integrity. Speed of the leader speed of the pack. Leaders are toxic, microagressions galore. Men are rewarded for mediocre efforts and achievements, while women and people of color are passed over for promotions or rewards. One women created a new Route to market for Adobe Sign, bringing in over 200M in revenue, while her male counterpart b...
Don't have any to be honest All positive things

- Lower Management is not good and they work to save their job. If needed they ruthlessly treat you bad. - Be aware of people who are with Adobe for a long period of time. They are there because they know the process and know how to keep themselves safe. - There is big disconnect with what upper management is saying v/s lower management. Upper management tries to connivence to be innovative, take risk and so on.. Bu...
Do not expect any promotions and advancement in your career. You get a role with negotiation and that's all. For next 6-8 years do not expect anything. Perks and Benefits are good

It's wild west. Official processes are often ignored. PM, Design, and Engineering often operate in parallel. Too much churn. Too many needless inefficiencies. Too many silos. A handful of senior leaders that are more interested in building a personal kingdom than good products. Wonderful people, mediocre work
-Too many tools -No standardization when it comes to workflows and processes -Difficult to move up -Pay doesn't match the rate of inflation Great company to work for
Many old people, not similar with the cure best practices and how to do things, can't speak up for something if you're junior Stock is in a free fall Going downhill

- nepotism toward Indian - low salary but you got chance to enjoy the next level of toxic culture from Amazon - have to work 7 days a week 12 hours per day, otherwise you will get pip or silent layoff - top performers who actually producing wonât get promoted. You have to be Indian, and learn how to backstabbing, politics is the key to success here Toxic culture and incompetent management

Management/leadership culture is quite toxic and politics-filled/with favoritism and toxic strategies being rewarded. Have not seen great managers/directors, only great ICs. Most managers/directors have to play the toxic game to get ahead. Good company to grow long term
Toxic cultures, backstabbing coworkers, poor leadership, pip culture .

Some groups could be tough to work at. Could be very hierarchical Overall a good company
